product_summary.one_sentence_summary: Online German sprint for international nurses preparing for German healthcare work and nursing-specific language proof.
product_summary.target_user: International nurses and recruitment partners preparing nurses for German practice.
product_summary.core_pain: Candidates need nursing-specific German fast enough to clear accepted proof and move into the German labour market.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: Faster progress toward a passable nursing German exam and improved employability in Germany.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The source card is written around the nurse's personal migration outcome, even though agencies can also buy it.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: The card explicitly mentions white-label use for agencies.
Hospitals and care facilities can indirectly buy the course to speed onboarding.

budget_owner.reason: The learner, the agency and the hiring employer can all be budget owners depending on the route.

buyer_influencers.reason: Recognition, visa and language-proof rules determine whether the route works.
Recruitment teams and L&D teams care about whether the nurse can be onboarded quickly.
Nursing managers influence whether a language sprint is good enough for the workplace.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: telc Deutsch B1-B2 Pflege exists as a nursing-specific exam that proves language competence at B1 and B2.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: telc states that the exam certifies reading, listening, writing and speaking at B1 and B2 for nursing staff.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Goethe-Test PRO Pflege exists as an accepted B2 nursing-language proof route.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Goethe states that the exam is recognised by BAMF and the German Foreign Office as B2 proof.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Germany's nursing sector had about 35,000 vacancies in 2023.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The Federal Employment Agency explicitly reported 35,000 registered vacancies in the nursing sector on average in 2023.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: About 270,000 qualified nurses in Germany were foreign nationals, about 16% of the workforce in 2023; 18% in 2025.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: BA reported 270,000 foreign-national nurses and 16% in 2023, then 18% in 2025 for nursing professions overall. The source card's 300k+ figure is not supported.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: BAMF-backed vocational German language support exists as a public route relevant to this product.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The DeuFöV regulation assigns implementation of occupational German language support to BAMF.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Bilateral agreements with India, Morocco and Brazil define the recruiting route.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The reviewed public sources show Triple Win arrangements with India, Indonesia, the Philippines and Tunisia, not Morocco or Brazil.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: A 8-12 week sprint is enough for most nurses to reach the required route proof.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The public sources confirm the route and exam types, but not that this exact sprint length is sufficient for most learners.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: 300K+ foreign nurses already work in Germany (17.8%).
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The reviewed official figures are lower and time-specific; the card's number is not confirmed as stated.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: No strong online-first healthcare German brand exists.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The reviewed evidence shows strong substitutes and recognized exam routes, but not an exhaustive market census.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The family-income framing is a reliable conversion lever.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: This is a commercial hypothesis, not an external fact.

regulator_entities.name: Federal Employment Agency (BA)
regulator_entities.relevance: Publishes shortage analysis and participates in Triple Win and labour-market support for nursing recruitment.

regulator_entities.name: BAMF
regulator_entities.relevance: Implements occupational German language support through DeuFöV.

framework_entities.name: DeuFöV
framework_entities.relevance: German regulation for occupation-related German language support.

framework_entities.name: Skilled Immigration Act
framework_entities.relevance: Part of the migration framework that makes nursing recruitment and recognition pathways important in Germany.

funding_bodies.name: BAMF
funding_bodies.relevance: Funds or organizes vocational German language support through DeuFöV.

funding_bodies.name: Federal Employment Agency (BA)
funding_bodies.relevance: Supports nursing retraining and recruitment programmes, including Triple Win and labour-market support.

approval_entities.name: telc Deutsch B1-B2 Pflege
approval_entities.relevance: Recognized nursing-language exam route that can be used as accepted proof.

approval_entities.name: Goethe-Test PRO Pflege
approval_entities.relevance: Recognized nursing-language exam route that is accepted by BAMF and the German Foreign Office.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: International nursing staff need B2-level language proof for nursing work in Germany.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: Foreign-trained nurses seeking to work in Germany

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Triple Win recruits nurses from India, Indonesia, the Philippines and Tunisia, and provides language support up to B1 in the country of origin.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: Nursing recruitment via the Triple Win programme

subsidies_or_funding.name: BAMF vocational German language support
subsidies_or_funding.eligibility_notes: Relevant public support exists, but the exact fit for this private sprint needs validation.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: telc Deutsch B1-B2 Pflege
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Nursing-specific exam route with B1/B2 language proof.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: Goethe-Test PRO Pflege
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Recognized B2 nursing-language exam route accepted by BAMF and the German Foreign Office.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: Triple Win programme
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: A government-backed recruitment and language-prep route for nurses from selected countries.

legal_claim_severity.reason: The product depends on regulated-profession language proof and placement-adjacent claims.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: Credible delivery requires nursing-pathway expertise, exam knowledge and likely recruitment/domain support.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Claims about accepted language proof and employability depend on recognized exam and route logic.

proof_stack.reason: The product must prove that learners can pass an accepted nursing-language route.
Exam pass is the core intermediate signal for this product.
Agencies and employers still have to buy the format rather than a cheaper generic German course.
The downstream value is work in Germany, not just course completion.

proof_burden.reason: The market cares about accepted language proof and actual hiring outcomes, not just attendance.

dependency_types.reason: Nursing work depends on recognition and language proof pathways.
The course must line up with accepted exam and immigration routes.
The card's white-label motion and recruitment angle depend on partner channels.
Healthcare and migration content needs specialist review.
The origin-country and destination-country path changes the support design.
Public funding routes exist and can affect buying decisions.

scope_variance_risk.reason: Even inside Germany, origin-country and employer route differences materially affect delivery.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: Expanding to other professions or destination countries would change the route logic materially.

subsidy_exists.reason: Public vocational German language support and nursing-recruitment programmes exist.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: The public support exists, but exact eligibility for this sprint depends on learner status and programme structure.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: The source card shows no subsidy, but public vocational language support and nursing-recruitment funding routes do exist.

market_regulatory_context.reason: This sits inside a regulated-profession and migration route where language proof is a gate, not a nice-to-have.

claim_language_risk.reason: The product sits next to accepted-language-proof claims and should not imply guaranteed placement or licensing.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: Delivery and claims depend heavily on external exam and migration routes.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: Strong substitutes already exist through telc, Goethe and government-backed nursing recruitment programmes.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: The source card's 300k+ nurse figure is not supported, while the official shortage data are lower and time-specific.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: Public funding routes exist even though the card says none.

positioning_risk.reason: The product can drift into a route-orchestration business if it promises exam, visa and placement outcomes together.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: Bilateral agreements with India, Morocco and Brazil define the recruiting route.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: The reviewed public evidence shows partner-country recruitment for India, Indonesia, the Philippines and Tunisia, not Morocco or Brazil.
Official BA figures are 270,000 foreign-national nurses in 2023 and 18% in 2025; the source-card number is not exact.
BAMF vocational German language support and BA-supported recruitment/training routes exist, so the blanket no-subsidy framing is incomplete.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Decide whether the product is exam prep only or a broader migration-support motion.
The public route differs by source country; do not reuse an unsupported country list.
Validate whether any BAMF or BA route is operationally usable for this offer.

sources.title: Nursing staff in Germany: mostly female, part-time and in more demand than ever before
Informationen zu den Prüfungsergebnissen - Goethe-Test PRO Pflege Deutsch für Pflegeberufe
Recruiting nursing professionals from abroad for the German labour market
Current projects for recruiting skilled workers
